A note with markdown-formatted body that can link to other items

content.title: My First Note
content.description:
# Welcome to Markdown Notes!

This is a note with **markdown** support.

## Features

- Write in markdown
- Link to other items using `item://` protocol
- Click Edit to modify

## Try It

Create another note and link to it like this:

`
[Link text](item://paste-item-id-here)
`

To get an item's ID, navigate to it and click "Copy ID" in the nav bar.

[Hobson](item://c3f4c6ad-e827-4999-bf22-724873a5aade)

content.title: Hobson
content.description: **Hobson** (this app) is a browser-based, always-on runtime environment that embodies radical structural uniformity: all entities (notes, code modules, type definitions, renderers, relationships) are "items" with identical structure, distinguished only by their type hierarchy rooted in a self-referential [atom](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000). The system bootstraps from minimal seed items into a fully self-extensible environment where modifications to code items immediately affect running behavior without restart, and where the boundary between "using" and "programming" the system dissolves—code is written in items, executed by the kernel's module loader, and can query, create, and render other items through a composable renderer architecture. Storage (IndexedDB) and execution (ES modules) are entirely local, enabling complete offline functionality while maintaining the immediacy and inspectability of Smalltalk-like image-based systems, but built with standard web technologies for true cross-platform deployment.
